title: Fix silent Type::Real default for fully-unbounded Range in test builders
analysis: The `infer_value_type` function in `crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs` (line 49-56) handles `Value::Range { lower, upper, .. }` by chaining `lower.map(infer_value_type).or_else(|| upper.map(infer_value_type)).unwrap_or(Type::Real)`. When both bounds are `None` (fully-unbounded range), this silently defaults to `Type::Real`, violating the project convention against silent defaults (feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md). The `Value::Tensor` arm at line 44-46 already correctly panics with a descriptive message directing users to `CompiledExpr::literal(value, type)`. The fix mirrors this pattern for Range. Only one file is affected since both the implementation and tests live in `builders.rs`. No callers currently construct fully-unbounded ranges via `literal()`, so this is a safety net against future misuse.

steps.description: Write a `#[test]` `#[should_panic(expected = "literal() cannot infer Range element type")]` test named `literal_fully_unbounded_range_panics` that calls `literal(Value::Range { lower: None, upper: None, lower_inclusive: false, upper_inclusive: false })`. This test will FAIL because the current code silently returns Type::Real instead of panicking. Place it in the existing `mod tests` block near line 715, after the other literal_* tests.

In the `Value::Range` arm of `infer_value_type` (line 54), replace `.unwrap_or(Type::Real)` with `.unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("literal() cannot infer Range element type for fully unbounded range. Use CompiledExpr::literal(value, type) directly."))`. This mirrors the Tensor arm's panic pattern at line 45. The step-1 test now passes.

Add positive test `literal_range_with_lower_bound_infers_type` verifying `literal(Value::Range { lower: Some(Box::new(Value::Int(1))), upper: None, lower_inclusive: true, upper_inclusive: false })` produces `Type::Range(Box::new(Type::Int))`. Ensures the lower-bound inference path still works.

Add positive test `literal_range_with_upper_bound_only_infers_type` verifying `literal(Value::Range { lower: None, upper: Some(Box::new(Value::Real(5.0))), lower_inclusive: false, upper_inclusive: true })` produces `Type::Range(Box::new(Type::Real))`. Covers the or_else fallback branch (upper bound used when lower is None).

design_decisions.decision: Use `unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(...))` instead of restructuring the match arm
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the existing chain structure (lower → upper → panic) readable and minimally invasive. The panic only triggers when both bounds are None, preserving the valid inference paths intact.

design_decisions.decision: Panic message mirrors the Tensor arm's wording pattern
design_decisions.rationale: Consistency with the existing Tensor panic at line 45: 'literal() cannot infer X type (reason). Use CompiledExpr::literal(value, type) directly.' Both direct users to the explicit-type alternative.

design_decisions.decision: Add positive tests for lower-only and upper-only Range bounds alongside the panic test
design_decisions.rationale: The change modifies a code path handling three cases (lower present, upper present, neither). Testing both valid paths ensures no regression from the change, and the panic test covers the invalid third case.

reuse.how: Mirror the panic message format: 'literal() cannot infer X type (reason). Use CompiledExpr::literal(value, type) directly.'

reuse.what: Existing literal_* test patterns in mod tests
reuse.how: Follow the same structure: construct a Value, call literal(), assert on result_type. Add #[should_panic] for the panic test.
Construct Range values directly in tests using Value::Range { lower, upper, lower_inclusive, upper_inclusive } matching the struct definition.
